strengths: 15+ years fullstack experience well exceeds the 6+ year requirement, with TypeScript/Node.js/React/MongoDB/AWS stack matched at Optilyz
Current re:cinq role explicitly covers AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic workflow frameworks — directly matching the AI agent development requirement
Strong engineering craftsmanship signals: TDD, pairing, CI/CD, trunk-based development at RIDE; teaches workshops on testing, web internals, and Selenium
concerns: No mention of Nest.js anywhere in the resume despite it being part of the primary stack
Kotlin is not listed; only Java appears in the skills and historical roles (robotic arms at Altom)
Vitest specifically is not cited; testing experience is generic (TDD, Selenium, custom D-lang frameworks) rather than tied to the required JS testing toolchain

key_factors.factor: TypeScript + Node.js + React + AWS + MongoDB stack match

response.justification: Candidate clears the seniority bar comfortably (Staff/Senior titles across 10+ years) and hits the hardest-to-source requirement: hands-on AI agent infrastructure work including MCP code generators at re:cinq. Primary product stack alignment is strong — TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, AWS CDK are all demonstrated at Optilyz on a production direct-mail platform. Cloud, Docker, Kubernetes, and Git are all evidenced (GISCollective deploys to k8s; dev-ops at Altom). Gaps are framework-specific rather than conceptual: Nest.js is absent and only Java (not Kotlin) is shown, but these are learnable in weeks for an engineer with this depth. Significant D-lang focus in mid-career roles is a mild signal-to-noise concern but offset by the recent TypeScript-heavy work.
